scope: Tier-1 fixes (T1.1, T1.2, T1.3) + Tier-3 Sanskrit falsification (T3)

t1_1_tight_renderings.test_name: Tight rendering set: re-run A1 + A5 + A6
t1_1_tight_renderings.verdict: T1.1 tightened renderings: position-match rate dropped from 102 (loose, 15 tokens) to 102 (tight, 8 tokens). Enmenanak score went from 7.0 -> 5.0. A1 p-value (tight) = 0.0707. A5 BH-FDR survivors (tight) = 0/30. A6 Ur III rate 6.69% vs OBab 13.27% (delta +6.58pp).

t1_2_meluhha_v2.verdict: T1.2 (A3-v2) token-level Meluhha co-occurrence: of 102 position-matched PNs (tight renderings), 0 have a >=3-segment contiguous match in at least one of the 180 Meluhha-mentioning tablets. Zero substantive co-occurrences. Either (a) Phase-29 candidates are not Meluhhan names, or (b) the corpus does not contain them, or (c) the matcher needs further relaxation.

t1_3_signid_audit.verdict: T1.3 sign-ID audit: M77 corpus has 64 distinct signs (5361 tokens). Phoneme map has 33 entries. Direct ID overlap = 2; via crosswalk = 2; UNION = 2. Phoneme coverage of M77 token frequency = 10.18%. This confirms the Phase-30a A8 finding: even with proper sign-ID alignment, only 2 signs in the M77 corpus have a phoneme reading. Phase-30a A8 cannot be meaningfully re-run until the phoneme map expands to cover more M77 corpus signs (P30-B2 Wells 2015 + P30-D1/D2 Wells/Fuls cross-cluster).

t3_yajnadevam.test_name: Yajnadevam Sanskrit vs Parpola Dravidian falsification (E7 head-to-head)
t3_yajnadevam.outcome: PARPOLA EDGE: Both maps significant but Dravidian scores higher (24.5 vs 17.5). Both hypotheses receive non-trivial iconographic-anchor support; Parpola's edge suggests Dravidian-specific homonymies (miin = fish/star, vaTa = banyan/north, piLLai = squirrel/child) ARE doing real work.
t3_yajnadevam.verdict: T3 falsification: Parpola Dravidian map observed anchor score = 24.5 (p=0.0001); Yajnadevam Sanskrit map observed = 17.5 (p=0.0001). Score difference = +7.00 in favor of Parpola. PARPOLA EDGE: Both maps significant but Dravidian scores higher (24.5 vs 17.5). Both hypotheses receive non-trivial iconographic-anchor support; Parpola's edge suggests Dravidian-specific homonymies (miin = fish/star, vaTa = banyan/north, piLLai = squirrel/child) ARE doing real work.

decision_summary: T1.1 PARTIAL: tight renderings drop position-match rate from 102 to 102; A5 still 0/30. A1 p=0.070693.
T1.2 FAIL: 0 PNs co-occur with Meluhha keyword even with token-level matching.
T1.3 CONFIRMS DATA LIMIT: M77 ↔ phoneme-map overlap = 2 (data-starved).

T3: PARPOLA EDGE: Both maps significant but Dravidian scores higher (24.5 vs 17.5). Both hypotheses receive non-trivial iconographic-anchor support; Parpola's edge suggests Dravidian-specific homonymies (miin = fish/star, vaTa = banyan/north, piLLai = squirrel/child) ARE doing real work.
next_action: PROCEED CAUTIOUSLY: Parpola wins Sanskrit head-to-head (score 24.5 vs 17.5, p=0.0001), but Tier-1 fixes did not fully restore A5/A3 power. Phase-30c should focus on Tier-2 corpus expansion (Fuls + Wells), then re-run Phase-30a tests at expanded scale.
